Title: The entry reaction of the plant shikimate pathway is subjected to highly complex metabolite-mediated regulation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry reaction / committed step of shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and D-erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP, the first committed intermediate of the shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aromatic amino acids and downstream aromatic natural products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed entry step and key control point of shikimate-pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid production; loss causes hypersensitivity to tyrosine and reduced high light-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially abaxial leaf surfaces under high light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in mature leaves and roots; induced by stresses according to expression databases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Major DHS transcript in mature leaves and roots; lower than AthDHS2 in young etiolated/de-etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light stress; exogenous tyrosine; aromatic amino acid feeding
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AthDHS1 promoter in complementation; pET28a expression construct in E. coli for recombinant assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA knockout mutant analysis (dhs1, SALK_055360); native-promoter complementation; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling (LC-MS, GC-MS); anthocyanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AthDHS1 encodes an active Arabidopsis DHS isoform catalyzing the entry reaction of the shikimate pathway. 2. Unlike AthDHS2, AthDHS1 was not inhibited by tyrosine or tryptophan and was slightly activated by them. 3. AthDHS1 was strongly inhibited by chorismate and caffeate, while arogenate counteracted chorismate inhibition. 4. dhs1 mutants were hypersensitive to tyrosine and showed reduced anthocyanin accumulation under high light, indicating an in planta role in maintaining aromatic-metabolite production under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/expression database mining (eFP browser, ATTED/coexpression); metabolomics by LC-MS and GC-MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate target for deregulating shikimate-pathway entry without direct AAA feedback inhibition; could be combined with downstream pathway engineering to enhance aromatic amino acid-derived metabolites, though chorismate/caffeate inhibition remains a constraint.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces stress-induced phenylpropanoid accumulation and increases tyrosine sensitivity; deregulation may perturb aromatic metabolic homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AthDHS1 required Mn2+ and reducing conditions, had the highest catalytic efficiency among the three isoforms, and its presence attenuated AAA inhibition of AthDHS2 likely through oligomeric mixing/heterocomplex effects.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry reaction / committed step of shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and D-erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP, initiating shikimate-pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aromatic amino acids and downstream aromatic compounds; directly limits carbon entry into the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control isoform of the committed entry step; direct feedback-regulated DHS isoform
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tyrosine and tryptophan inhibit AthDHS2, restricting shikimate-pathway flux; loss confers resistance to tyrosine-mediated growth defects and allows higher glyphosate-induced shikimate accumulation under high tyrosine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole seedlings and shoots; shikimate measured in aerial tissues after glyphosate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor supply for tyrosine, phenylalanine, tryptophan and numerous aromatic specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in early developmental stages, especially 3-4-day-old etiolated/de-etiolated seedlings; lower in mature leaves; induced by changes in light conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominant DHS isoform in young seedlings; present at substantial but not dominant levels in mature leaves and roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tyrosine feeding; tryptophan feeding; glyphosate treatment; seedling developmental stage; light/de-etiolation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AthDHS2 promoter in complementation; pET28a expression construct in E. coli for recombinant assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays with IC50 determination; T-DNA knockout mutant analysis (dhs2, SALK_033389); native-gene complementation; tissue extract DHS assays; RT-qPCR; shikimate quantification after glyphosate; enzyme-mixing assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AthDHS2 is the only Arabidopsis DHS isoform strongly inhibited by tyrosine and tryptophan, with IC50 values around 230 and 225 µM, respectively. 2. AthDHS2 is also inhibited by chorismate, caffeate, shikimate, prephenate, and arogenate. 3. AthDHS2 is predominantly expressed in young seedlings, explaining why AAA inhibition is detectable in seedlings but not mature leaves. 4. Loss of AthDHS2 makes plants resistant to tyrosine-mediated growth inhibition and enhances glyphosate-induced shikimate accumulation under high tyrosine, providing in planta evidence for Tyr feedback inhibition of pathway entry.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/expression database mining (eFP browser, ATTED/coexpression); metabolomics by LC-MS and GC-MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target for releasing Tyr/Trp feedback control at pathway entry to boost aromatic amino acids and derived metabolites, especially in seedlings or developmental stages where DHS2 predominates.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removing feedback control may disturb amino-acid homeostasis and carbon allocation; endogenous loss altered tyrosine sensitivity phenotypes and could affect developmental/stress metabolic balance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AthDHS2 retained residual DTT-independent activity and showed E4P substrate inhibition at high concentrations. Its AAA sensitivity was attenuated when mixed with AthDHS1 or AthDHS3, likely reflecting oligomeric interactions.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry reaction / committed step of shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and D-erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP at the entry of the shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aromatic amino acids and downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the committed entry step and contributes to control of aromatic flux under stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports stress-induced phenylpropanoid accumulation; loss causes hypersensitivity to tryptophan and reduced high light-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low basal expression relative to AthDHS1/2 in mature tissues; stress inducible according to expression databases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript abundance than AthDHS1 and AthDHS2 in mature leaves and roots; not predominant in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light stress; exogenous tryptophan; aromatic amino acid feeding
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AthDHS3 promoter in complementation; pET28a expression construct in E. coli for recombinant assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA knockout mutant analysis (dhs3, SK2559); native-promoter complementation; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling (LC-MS, GC-MS); anthocyanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AthDHS3 encodes an active DHS isoform with catalytic properties similar to the other Arabidopsis DHSs. 2. AthDHS3 was not inhibited by tyrosine or tryptophan and was slightly activated by tryptophan. 3. AthDHS3 was strongly inhibited by chorismate and caffeate, and chorismate inhibition was offset by arogenate. 4. dhs3 mutants were hypersensitive to tryptophan and accumulated less anthocyanin under high light, supporting a role in sustaining phenylpropanoid induction under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/expression database mining (eFP browser, ATTED/coexpression); metabolomics by LC-MS and GC-MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reinforce stress-inducible aromatic flux and phenylpropanoid output, potentially in combination with AthDHS1 engineering to maintain flux without AAA inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss decreases high light-induced anthocyanins and quercetin derivatives; manipulating activity may alter aromatic metabolic partitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AthDHS3 required Mn2+ and DTT and could mask AthDHS2 AAA sensitivity in mixed-enzyme assays, suggesting combinatorial control among isoforms.
